page: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpldsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. The architecture is arm64, with a cache configuration consisting of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 128 MB L3 cache. It lacks hyperthreading, meaning there is exactly one thread per core. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 1890 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The server achieved top-tier (top 10%) results in PassMark memory score, PassMark database operations, and large-block memory bandwidth (both read and write). Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench is strong (top 25%), as is software compilation with Clang and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance remains average (middle 50%) across stress-ng, Geekbench, and bzip3 decompression. Large LLM inference (70B model) prompt processing is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 10%, though text generation for the same model is average.

From a resource density perspective, the D32plds_v6 offers a compute-heavy profile with a low memory allocation of 2.0 GB per vCPU, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require high parallel processing power without massive memory footprints. The inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage adds value for data-intensive tasks. The lack of x86 vector extensions (such as AVX or AMX) and dedicated GPUs limits its utility for advanced machine learning or heavy matrix multiplication. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and redis caching.
description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides 64.0 GB of RAM, representing 2.0 GB per core, and 1890 GB of local storage. It lacks dedicated GPUs, hyperthreading, and x86 vector extensions. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and static web serving throughput. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model LLM prompt processing are average to poor. The low memory-to-core ratio presents a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is suited for multi-threaded database operations, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, offering strong multi-core performance and top-tier database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32plds_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 32 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for multi-core compute workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated cores, local storage, and high-bandwidth memory for parallelized database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
64.0 GB system memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
Strong multi-core performance for software compilation and web serving
Lacks hyperthreading, dedicated GPUs, and x86 vector extensions
